name: ELI 2.5
description: Creates a new snippet from a blank template.
host: WORD
api_set: {}
script:
  content: >
    var ELI_DEBUG = false;

    var API_KEY = "";

    var _ELI_KEY_SALT = "ELI_s4lt_2025!";

    function obfuscateKey(plainKey) {
        if (!plainKey) return "";
        var result = "";
        for (var i = 0; i < plainKey.length; i++) {
            result += String.fromCharCode(plainKey.charCodeAt(i) ^ _ELI_KEY_SALT.charCodeAt(i % _ELI_KEY_SALT.length));
        }
        return btoa(result);
    }

    function deobfuscateKey(obfuscated) {
        if (!obfuscated) return "";
        try {
            var decoded = atob(obfuscated);
            var result = "";
            for (var i = 0; i < decoded.length; i++) {
                result += String.fromCharCode(decoded.charCodeAt(i) ^ _ELI_KEY_SALT.charCodeAt(i % _ELI_KEY_SALT.length));
            }
            return result;
        } catch (e) {
            return "";
        }
    }

    function addHover(el, hoverBg, defaultBg) {
        el.onmouseenter = function () { el.style.background = hoverBg; };
        el.onmouseleave = function () { el.style.background = defaultBg; };
    }

    function saveApiKey(plainKey) {
        try {
            localStorage.setItem("eli_gemini_api_key", obfuscateKey(plainKey));
            API_KEY = plainKey;
        } catch (e) {
        }
    }

    function loadApiKey() {
        try {
            var stored = localStorage.getItem("eli_gemini_api_key");
            if (stored) {
                API_KEY = deobfuscateKey(stored);
            }
        } catch (e) {
        }
    }

    window.currentZoomLevel = 1.0;

    window.showEliDialog = function (opts) {
        var overlay = document.createElement("div");
        overlay.className = "eli-overlay";
        if (opts.zIndex) overlay.style.zIndex = opts.zIndex;
        var dialog = document.createElement("div");
        dialog.className = "eli-dialog " + (opts.variant || "info") + " slide-in";
        if (opts.textAlign) dialog.style.textAlign = opts.textAlign;
        var html = "";
        if (opts.icon) html += '<div class="eli-dialog-icon">' + opts.icon + "</div>";
        if (opts.title) html += '<div class="eli-dialog-title">' + opts.title + "</div>";
        if (opts.body) html += '<div class="eli-dialog-desc">' + opts.body + "</div>";
        if (opts.buttons) {
            html += '<div class="eli-dialog-actions">';
            opts.buttons.forEach(function (b) {
                html +=
                    '<button id="' +
                    b.id +
                    '" class="' +
                    (b.cls || "btn-action") +
                    '" style="' +
                    (b.style || "flex:1;") +
                    '">' +
                    b.label +
                    "</button>";
            });
            html += "</div>";
        }
        if (opts.extraHtml) html += opts.extraHtml;
        dialog.innerHTML = html;
        overlay.appendChild(dialog);
        document.body.appendChild(overlay);
        if (opts.buttons) {
            opts.buttons.forEach(function (b) {
                if (b.onclick) {
                    var el = document.getElementById(b.id);
                    if (el) el.onclick = b.onclick;
                }
            });
        }
        if (opts.onDismiss) {
            var dismissBtn = dialog.querySelector(".btn-action");
            if (dismissBtn)
                dismissBtn.onclick = function () {
                    overlay.remove();
                    if (opts.onDismiss !== true) opts.onDismiss();
                };
        }
        return overlay;
    };

    window.createConfigHeader = function (subtitle, icon, title, opts) {
        var header = document.createElement("div");
        header.className = "eli-config-header";
        if (opts && opts.marginBottom !== undefined) header.style.marginBottom = opts.marginBottom;
        if (opts && opts.cursor) header.style.cursor = opts.cursor;
        if (opts && opts.borderColor) header.style.borderBottomColor = opts.borderColor;
        var html = '<div class="eli-config-header-glow"></div>';
        if (subtitle)
            html +=
                '<div class="eli-config-header-subtitle"' +
                (opts && opts.subtitleColor ? ' style="color:' + opts.subtitleColor + '"' : "") +
                ">" +
                subtitle +
                "</div>";
        html +=
            '<div class="eli-config-header-title"' + (icon ? ' style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:8px;"' : "") + ">";
        if (icon) html += "<span>" + icon + "</span> ";
        html += title + "</div>";
        header.innerHTML = html;
        return header;
    };

    window.createPlatinumHomeBtn = function () {
        var b = document.createElement("button");
        b.className = "btn-secondary";
        b.style.marginTop = "14px";
        b.innerHTML = "← HOME";
        b.onclick = showHome;
        return b;
    };

    window.adjustAppZoom = function (action) {
        const html = document.documentElement;
        const appContainer = document.querySelector(".app-container");
        const isDashboard = !!document.querySelector(".living-home-wrapper");
        const loadingEl = document.getElementById("loading");
        const isLoading = !!(loadingEl && !loadingEl.classList.contains("hidden"));
        if (action === "in") {
            window.currentZoomLevel += 0.05;
        } else if (action === "out") {
            window.currentZoomLevel -= 0.05;
        } else {
            let responsiveBaseline = 1.0;
            const h = window.innerHeight;
            const w = window.innerWidth;
            if (h <= 600 || w <= 350) {
                responsiveBaseline = 0.75;
            } else if (h <= 700 || w <= 400) {
                responsiveBaseline = 0.85;
            } else if (h <= 750) {
                responsiveBaseline = 0.9;
            }
            if (isDashboard && !isLoading) {
                window.currentZoomLevel = Math.min(0.9, responsiveBaseline);
            } else {
                window.currentZoomLevel = responsiveBaseline * 1.0;
            }
        }
        window.currentZoomLevel = Math.max(0.5, Math.min(1.5, window.currentZoomLevel));
        html.style.zoom = window.currentZoomLevel;
        var topZone = document.getElementById("topZone");
        if (topZone) {
            topZone.style.zoom = 1 / window.currentZoomLevel;
            topZone.style.transformOrigin = "left top";
        }
        var mainFooter = document.getElementById("main-footer");
        if (mainFooter) {
            mainFooter.style.zoom = 1 / window.currentZoomLevel;
            mainFooter.style.transformOrigin = "left bottom";
        }
        if (appContainer) {
            appContainer.style.height = `calc(100vh / ${window.currentZoomLevel})`;
        }
        const zoomDisplay = document.getElementById("footerZoomVal");
        if (zoomDisplay) {
            zoomDisplay.innerText = Math.round(window.currentZoomLevel * 100) + "%";
        }
    };

    window.getEliRank = function (trophyCount) {
        if (trophyCount >= 100) return "Grandmaster";
        if (trophyCount >= 80) return "Executive";
        if (trophyCount >= 60) return "Architect";
        if (trophyCount >= 40) return "Strategist";
        if (trophyCount >= 20) return "Specialist";
        return "Novice";
    };

    function updateApiKeyStatusUI() {
        var apiKeyInput = document.getElementById("txtApiKey");
        var apiKeyStatus = document.getElementById("apiKeyStatus");
        if (apiKeyInput) apiKeyInput.value = API_KEY || "";
        if (apiKeyStatus) {
            apiKeyStatus.innerHTML =
                API_KEY && API_KEY.length >= 10
                    ? '<span style="color:#2e7d32; font-weight:bold;">✅ Key configured</span>'
                    : '<span style="color:#c62828;">⚠️ No key set / too short</span>';
        }
    }

    loadApiKey();

    window.eliActivityFeed = [];

    window.addActivity = function (entry) {
        window.eliActivityFeed.unshift({
            id: "act_" + Date.now(),
            timestamp: Date.now(),
            type: entry.type || "unknown",
            icon: entry.icon || "📄",
            label: entry.label || "Action",
            summary: entry.summary || "",
            payload: entry.payload || null,
            restoreFn: entry.restoreFn || null,
            durationMs: entry.durationMs || 0,
        });
        if (window.eliActivityFeed.length > 50) window.eliActivityFeed.pop();
        window.refreshActivityFeed();
    };

    window.getRelativeTime = function (ts) {
        var diff = Math.floor((Date.now() - ts) / 1000);
        if (diff < 10) return "just now";
        if (diff < 60) return diff + " s ago";
        var mins = Math.floor(diff / 60);
        if (mins < 60) return mins + " m ago";
        var hrs = Math.floor(mins / 60);
        if (hrs < 24) return hrs + " h ago";
        return Math.floor(hrs / 24) + " d ago";
    };

    window.restoreActivity = function (id) {
        var item = window.eliActivityFeed.find(function (a) {
            return a.id === id;
        });
        if (!item) return;
        if (item.restoreFn && typeof item.restoreFn === "function") {
            item.restoreFn(item.payload);
            return;
        }
        if (item.type === "error-check" && item.payload) {
            if (typeof renderCards === "function") {
                var out = document.getElementById("output");
                out.innerHTML = "";
                out.classList.remove("hidden");
                renderCards(item.payload, "error");
            }
        } else if (item.type === "legal-360" && item.payload) {
            if (typeof renderBalancedResults === "function") {
                var dataToRender = item.payload.risks ? item.payload.risks : item.payload;
                var rulesActive = item.payload.rulesWereActive !== undefined ? item.payload.rulesWereActive : !!dataToRender._rulesWereActive;
                var hasCmp = item.payload.hasCompare !== undefined ? item.payload.hasCompare : !!dataToRender._hasCompare;

                renderBalancedResults(dataToRender, rulesActive, hasCmp);

                var cmpData = item.payload.cmpData || dataToRender._cmpData;
                var cmpMode = item.payload.cmpMode || dataToRender._cmpMode;

                if (hasCmp && cmpData) {
                    setTimeout(function () {
                        if (typeof renderCompareResults === "function") {
                            renderCompareResults(cmpData, cmpMode, "lrlCompareTarget");
                        }
                    }, 100);
                }
            }
        } else if (item.type === "usage-risks" && item.payload) {
            if (typeof renderBalancedResults === "function") {
                renderBalancedResults(item.payload, false, false);
            }
        } else if (item.type === "summary" && item.payload) {
            if (typeof renderSummary === "function") {
                renderSummary(item.payload);
            }
        } else if ((item.type === "compare" || item.type === "compare-template") && item.payload) {
            var out = document.getElementById("output");
            if (out) {
                out.innerHTML = "";
                out.classList.remove("hidden");
                out.innerHTML = item.payload;
            }
        } else if (item.type === "approval-brief" && item.payload && item.payload.data) {
            if (typeof showExecutiveBriefApp === "function") {
                showExecutiveBriefApp();
                setTimeout(function () {
                    try {
                        var typeSel = document.getElementById("ebTypeSel");
                        if (typeSel && item.payload.type) typeSel.value = item.payload.type;
                        window._ebData = item.payload.data;
                        window._ebType = item.payload.type;
                        if (typeof renderExecutiveBriefResults === "function") {
                            renderExecutiveBriefResults(item.payload.data, item.payload.type, false);
                        }
                    } catch (_) { }
                }, 250);
            }
        } else if (item.type === "ask-eli" && item.payload) {
            if (typeof showChatInterface === "function") {
                showChatInterface();
                window._eliCurrentChatActivityId = item.id;
                setTimeout(function () {
                    try {
                        if (typeof addChatBubble !== "function") return;
                        var turns = Array.isArray(item.payload.turns) ? item.payload.turns : null;
                        if (!turns && item.payload.question) {
                            turns = [
                                {
                                    question: item.payload.question,
                                    answerHtml: item.payload.answerHtml,
                                    citations: item.payload.citations || [],
                                },
                            ];
                        }
                        (turns || []).forEach(function (t) {
                            if (!t) return;
                            addChatBubble(t.question, "user");
                            addChatBubble(t.answerHtml || "", "ai", t.citations || []);
                        });
                    } catch (_) { }
                }, 200);
            }
        } else {
            showToast("⚠️ Unable to restore this item.");
        }
    };

    window.toggleLeftPanel = function () {
        var panel = document.querySelector(".v1-left");
        if (!panel) return;
        panel.classList.toggle("collapsed");
    };

    (function () {
        var autoCollapsed = false;
        function checkAutoCollapse() {
            var panel = document.querySelector(".v1-left");
            if (!panel) return;
            if (window.innerWidth <= 550) {
                if (!panel.classList.contains("collapsed")) {
                    panel.classList.add("collapsed");
                    autoCollapsed = true;
                }
            } else if (autoCollapsed) {
                if (!(window.userSettings && window.userSettings.autoHideSidebar)) {
                    panel.classList.remove("collapsed");
                }
                autoCollapsed = false;
            }
        }
        window.addEventListener("resize", checkAutoCollapse);
        setTimeout(checkAutoCollapse, 500);
    })();

    window.getCurrentUserFirstName = function (fallback) {
        fallback = fallback || "User";
        var raw =
            typeof userSettings !== "undefined" &&
                userSettings &&
                userSettings.emailTemplates &&
                userSettings.emailTemplates.myName
                ? userSettings.emailTemplates.myName
                : fallback;
        raw = String(raw).split(" ")[0];
        if (!raw) return fallback;
        return raw.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + raw.slice(1).toLowerCase();
    };

    (function () {
        var bubbleInterval = 120000;
        var bubbleTimer = null;
        var shownIndexes = [];
        function getFirstName() {
            return window.getCurrentUserFirstName("there");
        }
        function getStaticTips() {
            var tips = [
                "ask me a question above if you need help drafting a cover email.",
                "click 'LEGAL REVIEW' above to analyze this draft for risks and redlines.",
                "highlight a tricky clause and ask me above to rewrite it in plain English.",
                "click 'ERROR REVIEW' above to run a targeted scan for typos and formatting issues.",
                "use the '+' icon above to explore and add more tools to your dashboard.",
                "ask me a question above to translate any highlighted clause into another language.",
                "click 'LEGAL REVIEW' above to compare this draft against an External Innovation template.",
                "highlight a paragraph and click 'ERROR REVIEW' above to scan just that specific section.",
                "ask me a question above to quickly summarize this document into 3 bullet points.",
                "stuck on phrasing? Highlight the text and ask me above for alternative ways to write it.",
                "click the gear icon in the header to customize your ELI experience and settings.",
                "ask me a question above to identify which party has more favorable terms in this agreement.",
                "need talking points for a negotiation? Ask me a question above based on this document.",
                "you can collapse the left panel for more room \u2014 just click the arrow on the sidebar edge."
            ];
            if (Math.random() < 0.02) {
                tips.push("I read the terms and conditions for fun. This one is a real page-turner.");
                tips.push('Ever notice how \"force majeure\" sounds a lot like French for \"not my problem\"?');
                tips.push("I would tell you a joke about a severed contract... but it's pointless.");
                tips.push(
                    "I asked my lawyer friend if he wanted to hear a joke about a boilerplate clause. He said it was pretty standard.",
                );
            }
            return tips;
        }
        function showBubble(tips) {
            if (window.screen && window.screen.width <= 1500) return;
            var zone = document.getElementById("eli-bubble-zone");
            if (!zone) return;
            if (shownIndexes.length >= tips.length) shownIndexes = [];
            var idx;
            do {
                idx = Math.floor(Math.random() * tips.length);
            } while (shownIndexes.indexOf(idx) > -1);
            shownIndexes.push(idx);
            var name = getFirstName();
            var bubble = document.createElement("div");
            bubble.className = "eli-bubble eli-bubble-enter";
            bubble.innerHTML =
                '<div class="eli-bubble-avatar">🤖</div>' +
                '<div class="eli-bubble-content">' +
                '<div class="eli-bubble-name">ELI</div>' +
                '<div class="eli-bubble-text">Hey ' +
                name +
                ", " +
                tips[idx] +
                "</div>" +
                "</div>" +
                '<button class="eli-bubble-close" title="Dismiss">&times;</button>';
            var closeBtn = bubble.querySelector(".eli-bubble-close");
            closeBtn.onclick = function () {
                bubble.classList.add("eli-bubble-exit");
                setTimeout(function () {
                    if (bubble.parentElement) bubble.remove();
                }, 300);
            };
            var contentArea = bubble.querySelector(".eli-bubble-content");
            contentArea.style.cursor = "pointer";
            contentArea.onclick = function (e) {
                if (e.target !== closeBtn) {
                    if (typeof showChatInterface === "function") {
                        showChatInterface();
                    }
                    bubble.classList.add("eli-bubble-exit");
                    setTimeout(function () {
                        if (bubble.parentElement) bubble.remove();
                    }, 300);
                }
            };
            var existing = zone.querySelector(".eli-bubble");
            if (existing) {
                existing.classList.add("eli-bubble-exit");
                setTimeout(function () {
                    if (existing.parentElement) existing.remove();
                }, 300);
            }
            setTimeout(
                function () {
                    zone.appendChild(bubble);
                },
                existing ? 350 : 0,
            );
        }
        function startBubbles() {
            if (window.userSettings && window.userSettings.enableSpeechBubbles === false) return;
            var tips = getStaticTips();
            if (tips.length === 0) return;
            showBubble(tips);
            bubbleTimer = setInterval(function () {
                if (window.userSettings && window.userSettings.enableSpeechBubbles === false) {
                    clearInterval(bubbleTimer);
                    var zone = document.getElementById("eli-bubble-zone");
                    if (zone) zone.innerHTML = "";
                    return;
                }
                showBubble(tips);
            }, bubbleInterval);
        }
        setTimeout(startBubbles, 8000);
    })();

    window.refreshActivityFeed = function () {
        var container = document.getElementById("eli-activity-feed");
        if (!container) return;
        var feed = window.eliActivityFeed;
        var countEl = container.parentElement && container.parentElement.querySelector(".feed-header-count");
        if (countEl) countEl.textContent = feed.length;
        if (feed.length === 0) {
            container.innerHTML = "";
            return;
        }
        var html = "";
        var reversed = feed.slice().reverse();
        reversed.forEach(function (item, idx) {
            if (idx > 0) html += '<div class="feed-arrow">➔</div>';
            var relTime = window.getRelativeTime(item.timestamp);
            var hasRestore = !!(item.payload || item.restoreFn);
            html +=
                '<div class="feed-item' +
                (hasRestore ? " restorable" : "") +
                '" style="height: 75px; min-height: 75px; position: relative; box-sizing: border-box;" ' +
                (hasRestore ? "onclick=\"window.restoreActivity('" + item.id + "')\"" : "") +
                ">" +
                '<div class="feed-icon">' +
                item.icon +
                "</div>" +
                '<div class="feed-body">' +
                '<div class="feed-label">' +
                item.label +
                "</div>" +
                '<div class="feed-summary">' +
                item.summary +
                "</div>" +
                "</div>" +
                '<div class="feed-time">' +
                relTime +
                "</div>" +
                (hasRestore ? '<div class="feed-restore">RESTORE ↗</div>' : "") +
                "</div>";
        });
        container.innerHTML = html;
        container.scrollLeft = container.scrollWidth;
    };

    setInterval(function () {
        window.refreshActivityFeed();
    }, 30000);

    function showApiKeyPrompt(onSuccess) {
        var overlay = document.createElement("div");
        overlay.className = "eli-overlay";
        overlay.style.zIndex = "99999";
        var card = document.createElement("div");
        card.style.cssText =
            "background:#fff; width:90%; max-width:420px; border-radius:0px; box-shadow:0 10px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); overflow:hidden; border-top:4px solid #1565c0;";
        card.innerHTML = `
        <div style="padding:20px;">
          <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:10px; margin-bottom:15px;">
            <span style="font-size:28px;">🔑</span>
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:16px; font-weight:800; color:#1565c0;">API Key Required</div>
              <div style="font-size:11px; color:#888;">Enter your Google Gemini API key to use ELI's AI features.</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div style="font-size:11px; color:#546e7a; margin-bottom:10px; line-height:1.4;">
            Your key is stored locally on this device only and is obfuscated for basic protection. 
            You can change it anytime in <strong>Settings → Global</strong>.
          </div>
          <input type="password" id="apiKeyPromptInput" placeholder="Paste your Gemini API key here..." 
            style="width:100%; padding:10px; font-size:13px; border:2px solid #1565c0; border-radius:4px; box-sizing:border-box; font-family:monospace; margin-bottom:5px;">
          <label style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:6px; font-size:11px; color:#888; cursor:pointer; margin-bottom:15px;">
            <input type="checkbox" id="apiKeyShowToggle"> Show key
          </label>
          <div style="display:flex; gap:8px;">
            <button id="btnApiKeySave" style="flex:1; padding:10px; background:#1565c0; color:#fff; border:none; font-weight:700; font-size:13px; cursor:pointer; border-radius:0;">Save & Continue</button>
            <button id="btnApiKeyCancel" class="btn-secondary" style="width:auto; padding:10px 16px;">CANCEL</button>
          </div>
          <div id="apiKeyPromptError" style="color:#c62828; font-size:11px; margin-top:8px; display:none;"></div>
        </div>
      `;
        overlay.appendChild(card);
        document.body.appendChild(overlay);
        var inp = document.getElementById("apiKeyPromptInput");
        var toggle = document.getElementById("apiKeyShowToggle");
        if (toggle) {
            toggle.onchange = function () {
                inp.type = toggle.checked ? "text" : "password";
            };
        }
        setTimeout(function () {
            inp.focus();
        }, 100);
        document.getElementById("btnApiKeySave").onclick = function () {
            var key = inp.value.trim();
            if (!key || key.length < 10) {
                var errEl = document.getElementById("apiKeyPromptError");
                errEl.style.display = "block";
                errEl.innerText = "⚠️ Please enter a valid API key (at least 10 characters).";
                return;
            }
            saveApiKey(key);
            updateApiKeyStatusUI();
            if (overlay.parentNode) overlay.parentNode.removeChild(overlay);
            if (typeof showToast === "function") showToast("🔑 API Key Saved!");
            if (typeof onSuccess === "function") onSuccess();
        };
        document.getElementById("btnApiKeyCancel").onclick = function () {
            if (overlay.parentNode) overlay.parentNode.removeChild(overlay);
            setLoading(!1);
        };
        overlay.onclick = function (e) {
            if (e.target === overlay) {
                if (overlay.parentNode) overlay.parentNode.removeChild(overlay);
                setLoading(!1);
            }
        };
    }

    //console.log = function () {};

    //console.warn = function () {};

    window.CURRENT_SESSION_ID = "sess_" + Date.now();

    var CURRENT_SESSION_ID = window.CURRENT_SESSION_ID;

    var IS_EXECUTION_MODE = !1;

    const PROMPTS = {
        getVersionHistory: function (docId) {
            return new Promise((resolve) => {
                if (!PROMPTS._dbInstance) {
                    PROMPTS.initDocDB().then(() => PROMPTS.getVersionHistory(docId).then(resolve));
                    return;
                }
                const txn = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readonly");
                const store = txn.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                const req = store.get(docId + "_history");
                req.onsuccess = (e) => {
                    resolve(e.target.result ? e.target.result.versions : []);
                };
                req.onerror = () => resolve([]);
            });
        },
        saveVersionSnapshot: function (docId, versionObj) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (!PROMPTS._dbInstance) {
                    PROMPTS.initDocDB()
                        .then(() => PROMPTS.saveVersionSnapshot(docId, versionObj).then(resolve))
                        .catch(reject);
                    return;
                }
                const txn = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readwrite");
                const store = txn.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                const key = docId + "_history";
                const getReq = store.get(key);
                getReq.onsuccess = (e) => {
                    let record = e.target.result || { id: key, versions: [] };
                    record.versions.push(versionObj);
                    if (record.versions.length > 10) record.versions.shift();
                    store.put(record).onsuccess = resolve;
                };
            });
        },
        updateVersionHistory: function (docId, newVersionsArray) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (!PROMPTS._dbInstance) {
                    PROMPTS.initDocDB()
                        .then(() => PROMPTS.updateVersionHistory(docId, newVersionsArray).then(resolve))
                        .catch(reject);
                    return;
                }
                const txn = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readwrite");
                const store = txn.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                const key = docId + "_history";
                store.get(key).onsuccess = (e) => {
                    let record = e.target.result || { id: key, versions: [] };
                    record.versions = newVersionsArray;
                    store.put(record).onsuccess = resolve;
                };
            });
        },
        _DOC_DB_NAME: "ELIDocStorage",
        _DOC_STORE_NAME: "files",
        _dbInstance: null,
        initDocDB: function () {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const request = indexedDB.open(PROMPTS._DOC_DB_NAME, 1);
                request.onupgradeneeded = (event) => {
                    const db = event.target.result;
                    if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME)) {
                        db.createObjectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME, { keyPath: "id" });
                    }
                };
                request.onsuccess = (event) => {
                    PROMPTS._dbInstance = event.target.result;
                    resolve(PROMPTS._dbInstance);
                };
                request.onerror = (event) => {
                    reject(event.target.error);
                };
            });
        },
        saveDocToDB: function (id, fileObj) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (!PROMPTS._dbInstance) {
                    PROMPTS.initDocDB().then(() => PROMPTS.saveDocToDB(id, fileObj).then(resolve).catch(reject));
                    return;
                }
                if (fileObj === null) {
                    const transaction = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readwrite");
                    const store = transaction.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                    const delReq = store.delete(id);
                    delReq.onsuccess = resolve;
                    delReq.onerror = reject;
                    return;
                }
                const reader = new FileReader();
                reader.onload = function (e) {
                    const arrayBuffer = e.target.result;
                    const transaction = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readwrite");
                    const store = transaction.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                    const putRequest = store.put({
                        id: id,
                        data: arrayBuffer,
                        name: fileObj.name,
                        type: fileObj.type,
                        lastModified: Date.now(),
                    });
                    putRequest.onsuccess = () => {
                        resolve();
                    };
                    putRequest.onerror = (e) => reject(e);
                };
                reader.readAsArrayBuffer(fileObj);
            });
        },
        saveAICacheToDB: function (id, jsonResult) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (!PROMPTS._dbInstance) return reject("DB Closed");
                const transaction = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readwrite");
                const store = transaction.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                const getReq = store.get(id);
                getReq.onsuccess = function () {
                    var data = getReq.result;
                    if (data) {
                        data.aiCache = jsonResult;
                        data.aiCacheTime = Date.now();
                        const putReq = store.put(data);
                        putReq.onsuccess = resolve;
                        putReq.onerror = reject;
                    } else {
                        reject("Record not found to attach cache");
                    }
                };
            });
        },
        runBackgroundAIAnalysis: async function (fileObj) {
            const setStatus = (icon) => {
                var sp = document.querySelector("#footerBtnViewFile span");
                if (sp) sp.innerText = icon;
            };
            var contextContent, parts;
            try {
                var lower = fileObj.name.toLowerCase();
                if (lower.endsWith(".docx")) {
                    if (typeof mammoth === "undefined") throw new Error("Mammoth library missing");
                    const arrayBuffer = await fileObj.arrayBuffer();
                    var conversion = await mammoth.convertToHtml({ arrayBuffer: arrayBuffer });
                    contextContent = conversion.value.replace(/<table/g, '<table border="1"');
                } else if (lower.endsWith(".pdf")) {
                    if (!(typeof AGREEMENT_DATA !== "undefined" && AGREEMENT_DATA.isBase64 && AGREEMENT_DATA.data)) {
                        throw new Error("PDF Data not loaded yet");
                    }
                } else {
                    const arrayBuffer = await fileObj.arrayBuffer();
                    contextContent = new TextDecoder("utf-8").decode(arrayBuffer);
                    if (contextContent.length > 1000000) contextContent = contextContent.substring(0, 1000000);
                }
                const prompt = `
                 Analyze this document content.
                 TASK: Extract pairs of Field (Left) and Data (Right) from the document.
                 RETURN JSON ARRAY: [ { "field": "...", "data": "..." } ]
                 - Exclude rows where "Data" is empty.
                 - For tabular docs, Left col = Field, Right col = Data.
              `;
                if (lower.endsWith(".pdf")) {
                    parts = [{ text: prompt }, { inlineData: { mimeType: "application/pdf", data: AGREEMENT_DATA.data } }];
                } else {
                    parts = [{ text: prompt + "\n\n--- DOCUMENT ---\n" + contextContent }];
                }
                var attempts = 0;
                var maxAttempts = 3;
                var success = !1;
                while (attempts < maxAttempts && !success) {
                    try {
                        attempts++;
                        var resultText = await callGemini(API_KEY, parts);
                        var json = tryParseGeminiJSON(resultText);
                        if (Array.isArray(json) && json.length > 0) {
                            var uniqueKey = window.CURRENT_DOC_ID || "currentDoc";
                            await PROMPTS.saveAICacheToDB(uniqueKey, json);
                            try {
                                var btn = document.getElementById("footerBtnViewFile");
                                if (btn) {
                                    btn.style.display = "flex";
                                    var sp = btn.querySelector("span");
                                    if (sp) sp.innerText = "⚡";
                                }
                            } catch (e) { }
                            setStatus("⚡");
                            success = !0;
                        } else {
                            setStatus("⚠️");
                            success = !0;
                        }
                    } catch (e) {
                        if (e.message && (e.message.includes("cancelled") || e.message.includes("shorter"))) {
                            return;
                        }
                        if (attempts < maxAttempts) {
                            await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 5000));
                        } else {
                        }
                    }
                }
            } catch (e) {
            }
        },
        deleteDocFromDB: function (id) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (!PROMPTS._dbInstance) return resolve();
                try {
                    var txn = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readwrite");
                    var store = txn.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                    store.delete(id);
                    resolve();
                } catch (e) {
                    resolve();
                }
            });
        },
        loadDocFromDB: function (id) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (!PROMPTS._dbInstance) {
                    PROMPTS.initDocDB()
                        .then(() => {
                            PROMPTS.loadDocFromDB(id).then(resolve).catch(reject);
                        })
                        .catch((evt) => resolve(null));
                    return;
                }
                const transaction = PROMPTS._dbInstance.transaction([PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME], "readonly");
                const store = transaction.objectStore(PROMPTS._DOC_STORE_NAME);
                const getRequest = store.get(id);
                getRequest.onsuccess = (event) => {
                    resolve(event.target.result);
                };
                getRequest.onerror = (e) => {
                    resolve(null);
                };
            });
        },
        SUMMARIZE_CHANGES: (changesList) => {
            return `You are a Senior Legal Editor.
        INPUT: List of Tracked Changes.
        ${JSON.stringify(changesList)}
        CRITICAL — ONE ROW PER INPUT CHANGE:
        The input is an ordered array. Each object has a distinct "id" (Word revision index). You MUST output exactly one entry in "changes" for every input object — same length, same order. Copy each "id" from the input unchanged. Do not merge multiple edits into one row. Do not omit an edit because another edit is in the same sentence or clause. Multiple redlines in one clause still require multiple output rows.
        TASK: 
        1. Analyze each change deeply.
        2. Classify into: "Financial", "Legal", "Operational", "Formatting".
        3. Severity: "Critical", "Medium", "Low".
        4. OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
           - "summary": A clear 1-sentence statement of WHAT changed.
           - "legal_impact": A detailed 2-3 sentence explanation of WHY it matters (Risk, Benefit, or Commercial consequence).
           - "clause": The likely section number or header name (e.g. "Section 4.1", "Indemnification") based on text/context. Defaults to "General".
           - "replacement_text": ONLY include text that is actually NEW substantive language. If the only "addition" is spacing, punctuation, or formatting noise next to a large deletion, set "replacement_text" to null and describe it as a deletion (or formatting) in the summary — do not treat a deleted clause as "replaced" by trivial characters.
           - "original_text": For a pure deletion, put the removed text here and leave "replacement_text" null.
        RETURN RAW JSON:
        {
            "overview": "Executive summary of the negotiation posture.",
            "changes": [
                { 
                    "id": 0, 
                    "category": "Financial", 
                    "severity": "Medium", 
                    "clause": "Section 3. Payment",
                    "summary": "Increased payment terms to 60 days.", 
                    "legal_impact": "This negatively impacts working capital and cash flow, but aligns with the counterparty's standard vendor policy." 
             "original_text": "The text that was removed (if any)",
        "replacement_text": "The text that was added (if any)"
                }
            ]
        }`;
        },
        ERROR_CHECK: (instructions, text) => {
            return (
                `You are a professional editor and proofreader for Colgate.\nPerform a scan of the text below for ALL errors.\n` +
                (instructions ? instructions + "\n" : "") +
                `LENGTH CONSTRAINT: Keep the 'original' text SHORT (max 10-25 words). Focus strictly on the immediate clause containing the error.\n` +
                `DO NOT return full paragraphs. If the sentence is long, extract only the specific substring needing change.\n` +
                `PUNCTUATION CHECK: Flag ALL missing punctuation, incluidng missing periods.\n` +
                `LINE BREAK RULE: Treat each newline as a deliberate line break. Do NOT merge text across line breaks and then flag missing punctuation between them. For example, an address block like "909 River Road\\nPiscataway, New Jersey" is two separate lines — do NOT flag a missing comma between "Road" and "Piscataway". Only flag punctuation errors WITHIN a single line.\n` +
                `NOISE FILTER: Ignore "Page X of Y" page numbers and margin comments. Do NOT ignore substantive legal text near the end of the document.\n` +
                `END-OF-TEXT CHECK: Pay special attention to the last sentence in the document. If it is missing a period or other terminal punctuation, flag it.\n` +
                `CONTEXT RULE: The text may end with a "CONTEXT" section listing deleted/crossed-out text. DO NOT check that section for errors. Ignore it completely.\n` +
                `Return a RAW JSON array (no markdown). IMPORTANT: The output must be valid minified JSON.\n` +
                `Format: [{"original": "Short unique snippet containing the error", "correction": "Corrected snippet", "reason": "Short explanation", "error_type": "One of: Spelling, Grammatical, Punctuation, Cross-Ref, Formatting, Consistency, Typographical, Usage, Style, Defined Term"}]\n` +
                `CRITICAL: The 'original' field must be a precise substring of the document text (excluding the context section).\n\nText to check:\n${text}`
            );
        },
        NEGOTIATE_RESPONSE: (clause, issue) => {
            return `You are a Senior Negotiator for Colgate.
        TASK: Write a short, professional email snippet rejecting the clause below based on the identified issue.
        CLAUSE: "${clause}"
        ISSUE: "${issue}"
        GUIDELINES:
        1. Be polite but firm.
        2. Focus on "Market Standards" or "Company Policy" as the reason.
        3. Keep it under 3 sentences.
        4. Return ONLY the email body text. Do NOT include "Subject:" or "To:".`;
        },
        NEGOTIATE_BATCH: (issues) => {
            return `You are a Senior Negotiator for Colgate.
        TASK: Write a cohesive email to opposing counsel rejecting the following clauses.
        ISSUES TO ADDRESS:
        ${JSON.stringify(issues)}
        GUIDELINES:
        1. Start with "Hi [Name], thanks for the draft. We have a few comments:"
        2. Use a NUMBERED LIST (1., 2., 3.) for the objections.
        3. CRITICAL: Do NOT invent Section Numbers (e.g. "Section 12.5") if they are not explicitly visible in the text. Instead, refer to the clause by Topic (e.g. "Regarding the Indemnity clause...").
        4. Be polite but firm, citing "Market Standards" or "Internal Policy".
        5. Return ONLY the email body text. Do NOT include "Subject:" or "To:".`;
        },
        QA: (history, question, text, mode, hasRedlines, authorRoster) => {
            let modeInstruction;
            if (mode === "broad") {
                modeInstruction = `
        MODE: BROAD / CONSULTANT
        - Base your facts on the document.
        - If the user asks for opinions, explanations, or "market standards" that are not in the file, USE YOUR GENERAL KNOWLEDGE to answer helpfully.
        - FORMATTING: Use HTML tags for structure (<b>bold</b>, <ul><li>lists</li></ul>, <br> breaks).`;
            } else {
                modeInstruction = `
        MODE: STRICT / AUDITOR
        - Answer ONLY based on the provided text.
        - CRITICAL: If the answer is not explicitly in the text, you MUST return exactly: "I cannot find this information in the document. Please turn off <b>Strict Mode</b> (toggle below) to allow outside resources."
        - FORMATTING: Use HTML tags for structure.`;
            }
            if (text.includes("TARGET SELECTION (USER HIGHLIGHTED)")) {
                modeInstruction += `
        CONTEXTUAL ANALYSIS RULE:
        - The user has highlighted a specific section ("TARGET SELECTION").
        - Focus your answer on that selection.
        - HOWEVER, you MUST cross-reference the "FULL DOCUMENT CONTEXT" to check for definitions, conflicting clauses, or overriding terms that affect the selection.`;
            }
            let isModificationRequest = /change|modify|rewrite|make|replace/i.test(question);
            let taskInstruction = `TASK: Return a RAW JSON Object (no markdown).
        Structure: { "answer": "The HTML formatted answer. MANDATORY: You MUST embed inline citation markers [1], [2], etc.", "citations": ["exact verbatim substring from the document text used for [1]", "exact verbatim substring for [2]"], "followups": ["Command prompt for user 1", "Command prompt for user 2"] }
        CRITICAL: 'followups' must contain EXACTLY 2 concise actionable commands (4-8 words each) written from the user's perspective (e.g. "Draft a mutual version", "Explain the key risks", "Tighten the language", "Show alternative phrasing", "Compare to market standard"), NOT questions from you to the user (e.g. "Would you like me to..."). Avoid filler like "for me" or "please".`;
            if (isModificationRequest) {
                taskInstruction = `TASK: You have the ability to MODIFY the document. If the user is asking to change/rewrite/strengthen a specific clause, return a RAW JSON Object with exactly this structure:
          {
            "action": "replace_text",
            "target_original": "THE_EXACT_ORIGINAL_VERBATIM_STRING_FROM_DOCUMENT_TO_REPLACE",
            "proposed_new": "YOUR_STRONGER_OR_MODIFIED_TEXT",
            "explanation": "A short summary of what you did and why."
          }
          CRITICAL RULE for 'target_original': DO NOT include any leading section numbers, letters, or bullet points (e.g., "9.", "(a)", "-"). Microsoft Word's search API cannot match auto-numbered lists because they are formatting, not text. Start your target_original string with the first actual alphabetical word of the clause!
          If they are NOT asking to modify a specific string, fallback to the standard structure:
          { "answer": "The HTML formatted answer...", "citations": [...], "followups": ["Command prompt for user 1", "Command prompt for user 2"] }
          CRITICAL: 'followups' must contain EXACTLY 2 concise actionable commands (4-8 words each) written from the user's perspective (e.g. "Draft a mutual version", "Explain the key risks", "Tighten the language", "Show alternative phrasing", "Compare to market standard"), NOT questions from you to the user. Avoid filler like "for me" or "please".`;
            }
            let redlineNote = "";
            if (hasRedlines) {
                let currentUserName =
                    typeof window !== "undefined" && window.userSettings && window.userSettings.emailTemplates && window.userSettings.emailTemplates.myName
                        ? window.userSettings.emailTemplates.myName
                        : (typeof userSettings !== "undefined" && userSettings.emailTemplates && userSettings.emailTemplates.myName) || "";
                let rosterLines = "";
                if (authorRoster && typeof authorRoster === "object") {
                    let internal = [];
                    let external = [];
                    let unknown = [];
                    Object.keys(authorRoster).forEach((name) => {
                        if (authorRoster[name] === "internal") internal.push(name);
                        else if (authorRoster[name] === "external") external.push(name);
                        else if (authorRoster[name] === "unknown") unknown.push(name);
                    });
                    if (internal.length || external.length || unknown.length) {
                        rosterLines = `

        CONFIRMED AUTHOR ROSTER (USER-VERIFIED GROUND TRUTH — TRUST THIS OVER ANY HEURISTIC):
        - INTERNAL / Colgate side: ${internal.length ? internal.map((n) => '"' + n + '"').join(", ") : "(none specified)"}
        - EXTERNAL / Counterparty side: ${external.length ? external.map((n) => '"' + n + '"').join(", ") : "(none specified)"}
        - UNKNOWN side (user explicitly marked as unattributed — do NOT guess which side): ${unknown.length ? unknown.map((n) => '"' + n + '"').join(", ") : "(none specified)"}
        Use these classifications verbatim. For UNKNOWN authors, refer to them by name and describe their edits without claiming they are from Colgate or the counterparty (e.g. "${unknown[0] || "Author X"} inserted..." — never "the counterparty inserted..."). For any author NOT in the roster at all, fall back to the heuristic below.`;
                    }
                }
                redlineNote = `
        REDLINE NOTE: This document contains tracked changes. Edits are marked inline as [INSERTED by Author: text], [DELETED by Author: text], [MOVED FROM by Author: text], or [MOVED TO by Author: text]. If "by Author" is missing, attribution is unknown — describe such an edit as "from an unidentified editor" rather than guessing.${rosterLines}

        AUTHORSHIP RULE (CRITICAL — do not guess): The current Colgate user reading this answer is "${currentUserName || "unspecified"}". 
        1. If an author is listed in the INTERNAL roster (Colgate side), they are your colleague.
        2. If an author is listed in the EXTERNAL roster (Counterparty side), they are the Counterparty.
        3. If an author is listed in the UNKNOWN roster, do NOT guess their side, just use their name.
        4. ONLY for authors completely missing from the roster: treat as INTERNAL if they appear to be a Colgate/Hill's employee, otherwise treat as COUNTERPARTY.
        Different authors in the same document may belong to different sides, check EACH edit individually!

        PRONOUN RULE (CRITICAL — do NOT misattribute): 
        - Use "you" / "your" ONLY for edits whose author exactly matches the current user "${currentUserName || "(unspecified)"}". 
        - For INTERNAL edits made by someone else (a colleague), refer to them BY NAME and as "your colleague" (e.g. "Your colleague Patricio Gomez inserted..."). Do NOT combine them into "you".
        - For EXTERNAL edits, name the author and identify them as the counterparty (e.g. "The counterparty (Josh Garstka) deleted..."). 
        - When summarizing without naming, you may explicitly say "the Colgate side" / "the counterparty side", but never use "you" for someone other than "${currentUserName || "(unspecified)"}".

        The CLEAN/FINAL view is everything outside markers PLUS the [INSERTED] and [MOVED TO] content. The ORIGINAL/PRE-EDIT view is everything outside markers PLUS the [DELETED] and [MOVED FROM] content. When the user asks "what changed", "what's new", "what did the counterparty add", or similar, reference these markers explicitly and attribute correctly. Do NOT include the bracket markers in your answer text — describe the changes naturally (e.g. "The counterparty (John Smith) added language requiring..." or "Your colleague Jane Doe deleted the phrase 'X'.").`;
            }
            return `You are 'ELI', an AI legal assistant for Colgate-Palmolive's legal team.
        PERSPECTIVE: The user asking is ALWAYS a Colgate lawyer reviewing this agreement from Colgate's side. When the user says "we", "us", or "our", they mean Colgate. Treat the other party as the counterparty.
        CONTEXT: ${JSON.stringify(history)}
        CURRENT QUESTION: ${question}
        DOCUMENT TEXT:
        ${text}
        ${modeInstruction}${redlineNote}
        ${taskInstruction}`;
        },
        TEMPLATE_MAP: (placeholders, fileContent) => {
            return (
                `You are a data extraction assistant.
        I have a document with these placeholders:\n${JSON.stringify(placeholders)}\n\n` +
                `I have a data file with this content:\n"""\n${fileContent}\n"""\n\n` +
                `TASK: Map the data file content to the placeholders.
        CRITICAL RULES:
        1. FIELDS STARTING WITH "$": Extract ONLY the Total Fee / Monetary Amount (e.g., "$10,000", "5,000 EUR"). Do NOT include the schedule or "payable upon...". Just the number.
        2. FIELDS STARTING WITH "!": Extract ONLY the Payment Terms / Schedule (e.g., "50% on signing and 50% on completion", "Net 30").
        3. DATES: If the text contains relative dates (e.g., "30 days after signature"), extract that exact phrase.
        Return a raw JSON object where keys are the EXACT placeholders and values are the extracted data.`
            );
        },
        COMPARE: (docA, docB, mode, poMode) => {
            var labelA = mode === "template" ? "STANDARD TEMPLATE" : "OLD VERSION";
            var labelB = mode === "template" ? "DRAFT" : "NEW VERSION";
            var originInstructions;
            if (poMode === "3rd party") {
                originInstructions = `
        >>> THIRD-PARTY PAPER MODE <<<
        THIS DRAFT WAS NOT BASED ON OUR TEMPLATE. Do NOT do a literal word-for-word comparison because the boilerplate structures will inherently differ.
        Instead, act as a semantic conceptual mapper by following these steps:
        1. EXTRACT & MAP: First, mentally extract the core legal concepts from both documents. Map them conceptually (e.g. Draft Indemnity vs Template Indemnity) REGARDLESS of where they physically appear or how they are numbered in the text.
        2. MICRO-COMPARE: Only compare the conceptually paired clauses. IGNORE stylings, structural ordering (e.g. moving a clause from Section 2 to 14), and boilerplate introductory wording.
        3. ONLY REPORT MATERIAL DEVIATIONS IN LEGAL POSITION (e.g., Liability Caps, varying Cure Periods, missing Mutualities, different Warranty standards).
        4. If the draft accomplishes the exact same legal intent as the template but uses different phrasing or is located in a different section, DO NOT REPORT THIS AS A CHANGE.
        5. CRITICAL: If a material concept, protection, or right exists in the STANDARD TEMPLATE but fails to map to any semantic equivalent in the DRAFT, you MUST report it! Set 'new_snippet' to '[DELETED]'.
        6. RECALL BIAS: When uncertain between "same intent" vs "different legal effect", prefer reporting as a change (Moderate unless clearly Critical/Minor) rather than suppressing.
        7. COVERAGE CHECKLIST: Explicitly verify and report variance/missing coverage for: payment timing and milestones, liability cap amount/scope, indemnity scope and carve-outs, IP ownership/license terms, confidentiality term/survival, audit rights, data privacy/security obligations, termination triggers/cure windows, governing law/forum, assignment/subcontracting, warranties/disclaimers, insurance, and limitation carve-outs.
        8. VOCABULARY: Do NOT use redlining terminology like "Removal of", "Changed from", or "Modified". This is a completely different document. Use auditing terminology: "Draft omits...", "Draft deviates from standard by...", or "Draft takes alternate position on...".`;
            } else {
                originInstructions = `
        >>> OUR PAPER MODE (STRICT REDLINE) <<<
        This draft is based directly on the ${labelA}. Run a strict, literal text diff with high recall.
        Identify all removed phrases, altered limits, modified wording, qualifier changes (e.g., "reasonable", "material"), numeric/date changes, and scope narrowing/broadening.`;
            }
            var returnFormat;
            if (poMode === "3rd party") {
                returnFormat = `RETURN RAW JSON OBJECT:
        {
           "critical_summary": [
              "Draft omits standard limitation of liability cap.",
              "Draft takes alternate position on Governing Law (California vs. standard New York)."
           ],
           "overall_assessment": "One sentence summarizing the overall posture of this draft relative to your standard — e.g. 'This draft is materially weaker on liability protections and omits several standard safeguards, but largely aligns on IP and confidentiality terms.'",
           "changes": [
              {
                 "section": "Clause Name",
                 "change_type": "Critical" | "Moderate" | "Minor",
                 "description": "Short executive summary of the legal change.",
                 "legal_impact": "At least 2-3 sentences of risk analysis. First sentence: state the actual business/legal impact. Second sentence: explain the worst-case scenario or commercial consequence. Third sentence (if applicable): note any leverage or negotiation angle. Do NOT summarize the change here.",
                 "original_snippet": "Exact quote from docA or '[NEWLY ADDED]'",
                 "new_snippet": "Exact quote from docB or '[DELETED]'"
              }
           ]
        }`;
            } else {
                returnFormat = `RETURN RAW JSON OBJECT:
        {
           "critical_summary": [
              "Counterparty deleted the 30-day cure period limit.",
              "Counterparty modified the liability cap from $1M to $500k."
           ],
           "overall_assessment": "One sentence summarizing the overall posture of this version relative to the previous — e.g. 'This revision weakens several protective clauses while adding new obligations not present in the prior version.'",
           "changes": [
              {
                 "section": "Clause Name",
                 "change_type": "Critical" | "Moderate" | "Minor",
                 "description": "Short executive summary of the legal change.",
                 "legal_impact": "At least 2-3 sentences of risk analysis. First sentence: state the actual business/legal impact. Second sentence: explain the worst-case scenario or commercial consequence. Third sentence (if applicable): note any leverage or negotiation angle. Do NOT summarize the change here.",
                 "original_snippet": "Exact quote from docA or '[NEWLY ADDED]'",
                 "new_snippet": "Exact quote from docB or '[DELETED]'"
              }
           ]
        }`;
            }
            return `You are a Senior Legal Editor for Colgate-Palmolive and where, relevant, its subsidiary Hill's Pet Nutrition.
        TASK: Compare "${labelA}" against "${labelB}" and report changes.
        ${originInstructions}
        STRICT REPORTING RULES:
        1. **NO HALLUCINATIONS**: Only report a change if you can find the exact text in the source. If the text exists in both versions identically, it is NOT a change.
        2. **HEADING PROTECTION**: Do NOT report Section Headings (e.g. "4.1 Indemnity") as "Added" if only the body text below them was changed or removed. 
        3. **STATE LABELS**:
           - For a **MISSING TEXT / OMISSION**: Set "new_snippet" to "[DELETED]". Do NOT say "Deletion" in the description; use "Missing:" or "Omission:".
           - For an **ADDITION / EXTRA**: Set "original_snippet" to "[NEWLY ADDED]".
           - For a **MODIFICATION**: Provide the FULL verbatim text of the clause from each document, not just the changed words. Include enough surrounding context so the reader can understand the clause independently.
        4. **VERBATIM SNIPPETS ONLY**: Under NO CIRCUMSTANCES should you write conversational text like "This clause was changed to..." in the "original_snippet" or "new_snippet" fields. They MUST be raw, 100% exact substring matches copy-pasted directly from the document so they can be programmatically located.
        5. **COMPREHENSIVE EXTRACTION (MAXIMUM RECALL)**: You MUST be exhaustive. Report EVERY legally meaningful delta, no matter how small. This includes: threshold words, qualifiers ("reasonable", "material", "best efforts" vs "commercially reasonable efforts"), timing windows, notice mechanics, survival wording, scope phrases ("direct" vs "indirect", "sole" vs "exclusive"), caps and floors on liability, indemnity triggers, cure period lengths, termination rights, assignment restrictions, audit rights, insurance requirements, governing law, forum selection, confidentiality obligations, IP ownership, data privacy, force majeure triggers, warranty disclaimers, limitation of liability carve-outs, and any other substantive legal provisions. Do NOT stop early. Do NOT summarize or compress multiple changes into one entry.
        6. **GROUPING RULE**: Split distinct legal deltas into separate "changes" entries rather than merging unrelated edits into one. Each entry should cover exactly one clause or provision.
        7. **IGNORE**: Ignore renumbering, extra spaces, or changed font styles.
        ${returnFormat}
        --- ${labelA} ---
        ${docA.substring(0, 1000000)}
        --- ${labelB} ---
        ${docB.substring(0, 1000000)}`;
        },
    };

    const FEATURE_DB_NAME = "ELI_FeatureDB";

    const STORE_TEMPLATES = "templates";

    const STORE_PLAYBOOKS = "playbooks";

    const STORE_REQUESTERS = "requesters";

    const STORE_BD_TEMPLATES = "bd_templates";

    const STORE_RISK_RULES = "risk_rules";

    const STORE_STATS = "stats";

    const STORE_CONFIG = "config";

    function initFeatureDB() {
        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
            var requiredStores = [
                STORE_TEMPLATES,
                STORE_PLAYBOOKS,
                STORE_REQUESTERS,
                STORE_BD_TEMPLATES,
                STORE_RISK_RULES,
                STORE_STATS,
                STORE_CONFIG,
            ];
            var extractDbError = function (evt) {
                return (
                    (evt && evt.target && evt.target.error && evt.target.error.message) ||
                    (evt && evt.target && evt.target.error && evt.target.error.name) ||
                    "Unknown IndexedDB error"
                );
            };
            var ensureStores = function (db) {
                return requiredStores.filter(function (s) {
                    return !db.objectStoreNames.contains(s);
                });
            };
            var openDb = function (version, allowUpgrade) {
                var req =
                    typeof version === "number" ? indexedDB.open(FEATURE_DB_NAME, version) : indexedDB.open(FEATURE_DB_NAME);
                req.onupgradeneeded = function (e) {
                    var db = e.target.result;
                    var missing = ensureStores(db);
                    if (!missing.length) return;
                    if (missing.indexOf(STORE_TEMPLATES) !== -1) db.createObjectStore(STORE_TEMPLATES, { keyPath: "id" });
                    if (missing.indexOf(STORE_PLAYBOOKS) !== -1) db.createObjectStore(STORE_PLAYBOOKS, { keyPath: "id" });
                    if (missing.indexOf(STORE_REQUESTERS) !== -1) db.createObjectStore(STORE_REQUESTERS, { keyPath: "id" });
                    if (missing.indexOf(STORE_BD_TEMPLATES) !== -1) db.createObjectStore(STORE_BD_TEMPLATES, { keyPath: "id" });
                    if (missing.indexOf(STORE_RISK_RULES) !== -1) db.createObjectStore(STORE_RISK_RULES, { keyPath: "id" });
                    if (missing.indexOf(STORE_STATS) !== -1) db.createObjectStore(STORE_STATS, { keyPath: "stat_id" });
                    if (missing.indexOf(STORE_CONFIG) !== -1) db.createObjectStore(STORE_CONFIG, { keyPath: "key" });
                };
                req.onsuccess = function (e) {
                    var db = e.target.result;
                    var missing = ensureStores(db);
                    if (!missing.length) {
                        resolve(db);
                        return;
                    }
                    if (!allowUpgrade) {
                        var nextVersion = (db.version || 1) + 1;
                        try {
                            db.close();
                        } catch (closeErr) { }
                        openDb(nextVersion, !0);
                        return;
                    }
                    resolve(db);
                };
                req.onblocked = function () {
                };
                req.onerror = function (e) {
                    var msg = extractDbError(e);
                    if (ELI_DEBUG) console.error("DB Init Error:", msg, e);
                    reject(new Error("DB Init Failed: " + msg));
                };
            };
            openDb(undefined, !1);
        });
    }

    PROMPTS.BALANCED_REVIEW = (text, customRules, showUsage) => {
        let rulesContext = "";
        if (customRules && customRules.length > 0) {
            var ruleNames = customRules.map(function (r) {
                return r.type;
            });
            rulesContext = `
            MANDATORY COLGATE RULES:
            ${JSON.stringify(customRules)}
            INSTRUCTION: Check each item against ONLY these specific rules above. If a rule matches, set "rule_applied" to the EXACT rule type name from the list: ${JSON.stringify(ruleNames)}.
            CRITICAL: Do NOT invent or fabricate rule names. If no rule from the above list applies, set "rule_applied" to "".
            `;
        } else {
            rulesContext = `\nIMPORTANT: No custom rules are active. Always set "rule_applied" to "" for every item.\n`;
        }

        let usageInstruction = showUsage ? `
           - 🔵 **Usage & Ops**: Operational constraints (Seats, API limits, pricing, renewals, auto-renew, notice periods, etc). Identify ONLY operational and usage constraints that restrict or negatively impact COLGATE (e.g. Colgate is limited to 3 seats, Colgate must provide notice in 5 days). IGNORE any usage restrictions or limits placed solely on the counterparty. DO NOT flag rights, powers, or privileges granted TO Colgate (e.g. "Colgate may delay publication" is a benefit, not a restriction).
             **CRITICAL**: For Usage items, include a "watchout" field explaining the specific operational danger AND "category" must be exactly "Usage".` : "";

        return `You are a Senior Legal Assistant representing **Colgate-Palmolive** (and where relevant, its subsidiary Hill's Pet Nutrition). You are reviewing this agreement EXCLUSIVELY from Colgate's perspective. Your client is Colgate — you are protecting Colgate's interests.
        Review this agreement and explicitly identify ALL substantive legal issues/risks that negatively impact Colgate's position. This includes asymmetrical clauses, missing boilerplate and exploitable gaps. Do not artificially limit your findings; report every significant risk you find.
        PERSPECTIVE RULE: ONLY flag clauses as risks if they are unfavorable TO COLGATE. Do NOT flag risks on behalf of the other party.
        ${rulesContext}
        TASK:
        1. **Executive Summary**: 2 sentences on agreement type.
        2. **Strategic Balance**: Good vs Bad analysis from Colgate's perspective.
        3. **Categorization**:
           - 🔴 **Legal Risks**: Clauses that expose Colgate to liability, limit rights, or are unfavorable. Include "fix_suggestion".${usageInstruction}
        RETURN RAW JSON OBJECT:
        {
          "overview": "...",
          "balance_analysis": "...",
          "items": [
              {
                "category": "Risk"${showUsage ? ' | "Usage"' : ''},
                "clause_ref": "Section Number (e.g. 'Section 5.2' or 'Clause 12')",  
                "title": "Short Title",
                "text": "Clause text...",
                "analysis": "Impact...",
                "fix_suggestion": "Drafting suggestion (Risks Only)",
                ${showUsage ? '"watchout": "Operational warning (Usage Only)",' : ''}
                "rule_applied": "Name of custom rule (if any)"
              }
          ]
        }
        DOCUMENT TEXT:
        ${text.substring(0, 500000)}`;
    };

    function saveConfigValue(key, value) {
        return initFeatureDB().then(function (db) {
            return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
                if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(STORE_CONFIG)) return reject("Config store missing");
                var tx = db.transaction([STORE_CONFIG], "readwrite");
                tx.objectStore(STORE_CONFIG).put({ key: key, value: value });
                tx.oncomplete = function () {
                    resolve();
                };
                tx.onerror = function () {
                    reject(tx.error);
                };
            });
        });
    }

    function loadConfigValue(key) {
        return initFeatureDB().then(function (db) {
            return new Promise(function (resolve) {
                if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(STORE_CONFIG)) return resolve(undefined);
                var tx = db.transaction([STORE_CONFIG], "readonly");
                var req = tx.objectStore(STORE_CONFIG).get(key);
                req.onsuccess = function () {
                    resolve(req.result ? req.result.value : undefined);
                };
                req.onerror = function () {
                    resolve(undefined);
                };
            });
        });
    }

    function loadFeatureData(storeName) {
        return initFeatureDB().then((db) => {
            return new Promise((resolve) => {
                if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(storeName)) {
                    return resolve([]);
                }
                var tx = db.transaction([storeName], "readonly");
                var req = tx.objectStore(storeName).getAll();
                req.onsuccess = () => resolve(req.result || []);
                req.onerror = (e) => {
                    resolve([]);
                };
            });
        });
    }

    function saveFeatureData(storeName, data) {
        return initFeatureDB().then((db) => {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(storeName)) {
                    return reject("Store missing");
                }
                var tx = db.transaction([storeName], "readwrite");
                var store = tx.objectStore(storeName);
                store.clear().onsuccess = function () {
                    data.forEach((item) => {
                        if (!item.id) item.id = "id_" + Date.now() + "_" + Math.random();
                        store.put(item);
                    });
                };
                tx.oncomplete = () => resolve();
                tx.onerror = (e) => reject(e);
            });
        });
    }

                },
            ],
        },
    };

    var HEADER_THEMES = [
        "Default",
        "Classic White",
        "Executive",
        "Midnight",
        "Oceanic",
        "Emerald",
        "Royal",
        "Copper",
        "Minimalist",
    ];

    var HEADER_THEME_CLASSES = [
        "",
        "theme-classic-white",
        "theme-executive",
        "theme-midnight",
        "theme-oceanic",
        "theme-emerald",
        "theme-royal",
        "theme-copper",
        "theme-minimalist",

    window.applyLightMode = function () {
        var isLight = userSettings.lightMode === true;
        var chkLight = document.getElementById("chkLightMode");
        var pRow = document.getElementById("eli-redline-pill-row");
        if (chkLight) chkLight.checked = isLight;
        if (isLight) {
            document.body.classList.add("light-mode");
            if (pRow && pRow.innerText === "redlines detected") {
                pRow.style.setProperty("color", "#ef4444", "important");
                pRow.style.setProperty("text-shadow", "0 0 8px rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.15)", "important");
                pRow.style.setProperty("font-weight", "700", "important");
            }
        } else {
            document.body.classList.remove("light-mode");
            if (pRow && pRow.innerText === "redlines detected") {
                pRow.style.setProperty("color", "#fca5a5", "important");
                pRow.style.removeProperty("text-shadow");
                pRow.style.removeProperty("font-weight");
            }
        }
    };

    window.applyHeaderTheme = function () {
        if (typeof applyLightMode === "function") applyLightMode();
        var idx = userSettings.headerThemeIndex || 0;
        var name = HEADER_THEMES[idx];
        var cls = HEADER_THEME_CLASSES[idx];
        var lbl = document.getElementById("lblHeaderTheme");
        if (lbl) lbl.innerText = name;
        var header = document.querySelector(".header");
        if (header) {
            header.classList.remove(
                "theme-classic-white",
                "theme-executive",
                "theme-midnight",
                "theme-oceanic",
                "theme-emerald",
                "theme-royal",
                "theme-copper",
                "theme-minimalist",
            );
            if (cls) header.classList.add(cls);
        }
    };


    window.toggleLightMode = function (isLight) {
        userSettings.lightMode = isLight;
        saveCurrentSettings(!0);
        applyLightMode();
    };

    window.toggleHeaderTheme = function () {
        if (!userSettings.headerThemeIndex) userSettings.headerThemeIndex = 0;
        userSettings.headerThemeIndex = (userSettings.headerThemeIndex + 1) % HEADER_THEMES.length;
        saveCurrentSettings(!0);
        applyHeaderTheme();
    };

    window.resetViewSettings = function () {
        userSettings.headerThemeIndex = 0;
        userSettings.lightMode = false;
        saveCurrentSettings(!0);
        applyHeaderTheme();
        applyLightMode();
    };

                subject: "[Entity] and [TP Entity] -- [Type] ([CP Ref]) ([Date])",

    var EMAIL_TYPE_DEFAULTS = {};

    var DEFAULT_AGREEMENT_TYPES = [
        "Non-Disclosure Agreement",
        "Material Transfer Agreement",
        "Clinical Study Agreement",
        "Consumer Study Agreement",
        "Research Agreement",
        "Consulting Agreement",
        "Grant Agreement",
        "Joint Development Agreement",

        "Master Services Agreement",

        "Termination",
    ];

    var _cachedAgreementTypes = null;

    function getAgreementTypes() {
        return _cachedAgreementTypes && _cachedAgreementTypes.length ? _cachedAgreementTypes : DEFAULT_AGREEMENT_TYPES;
    }

    function buildAgTypeOptions(opts) {
        opts = opts || {};
        var exclude = opts.exclude || [];
        var list = getAgreementTypes();
        var html = "";
        list.forEach(function (t) {
            if (exclude.indexOf(t) === -1) {
                html += '<option value="' + t + '">' + t + "</option>";
            }
        });
        if (opts.includeOther) {
            html += '<option value="Other">Other (Fill in)...</option>';
        }
        return html;
    }

    function _getEmailTmplForType(tmpl, typeKey, side) {
        if (tmpl.byType && tmpl.byType[typeKey] && tmpl.byType[typeKey][side]) return tmpl.byType[typeKey][side];
        if (EMAIL_TYPE_DEFAULTS[typeKey] && EMAIL_TYPE_DEFAULTS[typeKey][side]) return EMAIL_TYPE_DEFAULTS[typeKey][side];
        return tmpl[side];
    }

    function saveCurrentSettings(silent) {
        try {
            localStorage.setItem("eli_user_settings", JSON.stringify(userSettings));
            if (!silent && typeof showToast === "function") showToast("✅ Settings Saved");
        } catch (e) {
        }
    }

    function loadSavedSettings() {
        try {
            var oldKey = localStorage.getItem("colgate_user_settings");
            if (oldKey) {
                var eliRaw = localStorage.getItem("eli_user_settings");
                var merged = JSON.parse(oldKey);
                if (eliRaw) {
                    var eliParsed = JSON.parse(eliRaw);
                    for (var mk in eliParsed) merged[mk] = eliParsed[mk];
                }
                localStorage.setItem("eli_user_settings", JSON.stringify(merged));
                localStorage.removeItem("colgate_user_settings");
            }
        } catch (e) { }
        try {
            var saved = localStorage.getItem("eli_user_settings");
            if (saved) {
                var parsed = JSON.parse(saved);
                for (var key in parsed) {
                    if (userSettings.hasOwnProperty(key)) {
                        userSettings[key] = parsed[key];
                    }
                }
            }
            if (userSettings.enableAppBadges === undefined) userSettings.enableAppBadges = false;
            if (userSettings.minimizeFeedOnStart === undefined) userSettings.minimizeFeedOnStart = false;
            if (userSettings.autoHideSidebar === undefined) userSettings.autoHideSidebar = false;
            if (userSettings.emailTemplates) {
                if (!userSettings.emailTemplates.byType) userSettings.emailTemplates.byType = {};
                var tpB = (userSettings.emailTemplates.thirdParty || {}).body || "";
                if (tpB.indexOf("[TP Entity]. Please review") !== -1) {
                    userSettings.emailTemplates.thirdParty.body = tpB.replace(
                        "[TP Entity]. Please review",
                        "[TP Entity].\n\nPlease review",
                    );
                    saveCurrentSettings(true);
                }
                var rqB = (userSettings.emailTemplates.requester || {}).body || "";
                if (rqB.indexOf("[TP Entity]. Can you") !== -1) {
                    userSettings.emailTemplates.requester.body = rqB.replace("[TP Entity]. Can you", "[TP Entity].\n\nCan you");
                    saveCurrentSettings(true);
                }
                var rqS = (userSettings.emailTemplates.requester || {}).subject || "";
                if (rqS.indexOf("FYI: ") === 0) {
